volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Merchant (Section 2.3.385b - Section 2.3.448b)

sections.label: Section 2.3.385b
sections.translation: Nay , but stop ; I still want to make a few enquiries of you first .

sections.label: Section 2.3.386b
sections.translation: Say what it is you wish .

sections.label: Section 2.3.387
sections.translation: Have you all along been well ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.387b
sections.original: Perpetuo recte , dum quidem illic fui ;
sections.translation: Quite well all the time , so long , indeed , as I was there ; but as soon as I had arrived here in harbour , I don ’ t know what faintness it was came over me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.389
sections.original: Nausea edepol factum credo ; verum actutum abscesserit .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I suppose it arose from sea-sickness ; but it will be going off just now .

sections.label: Section 2.3.390
sections.original: sed quid ais ? ecquam tu advexti tuae matri ancillam e Rhodo ?
sections.translation: But how say you ? What servant-maid is this that you have brought from Rhodes for your mother ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.391
sections.translation: I ’ ve brought one .

sections.label: Section 2.3.391b
sections.translation: Well , what sort of a woman is she as to appearance ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.391c
sections.translation: Not an ill-favored one , i ’ faith .

sections.label: Section 2.3.392
sections.translation: How is she as to manners ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.392b
sections.translation: In my way of thinking , I never saw one better .

sections.label: Section 2.3.393
sections.original: Mihi quidem edepol visast , quom illam vidi .
sections.translation: So , indeed , i ’ faith , she seemed to me when I saw her .

sections.label: Section 2.3.393b
sections.translation: How now , have you seen her , father ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.394
sections.original: Vidi . verum non ex usu nostrost , neque adeo placet .
sections.translation: I have seen her ; but she doesn ’ t suit our ways , and so she doesn ’ t please me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.395
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.395b
sections.original: Quia † non nostra formam habet dignam domo .
sections.translation: Because she hasn ’ t a figure suitable to our establishment ; we stand in need of no female servant but one who can weave , grind , chop wood , make yarn , sweep out the house , stand a beating , and who can have every day ’ s victuals cooked for the household . This one will be able to do not any single one of these things .

sections.label: Section 2.3.399b
sections.translation: Why , in fact ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.400
sections.original: ea causa equidem illam emi , dono quam darem matri meae .
sections.translation: for this reason I purchased her , to make a present of her to my mother .

sections.label: Section 2.3.401
sections.original: Ne duas , neve te advexisse dixeris .
sections.translation: Don ’ t you be giving her , nor mention that you have brought her .

sections.label: Section 2.3.401b
sections.translation: The Deities favour me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.403
sections.original: Labefacto paulatim . verum quod praeterii dicere ,
sections.translation: I ’ m shaking him by slow degrees . ( Aloud .) But , what I omitted to say ,— she can neither with due propriety follow your mother as an attendant ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.405
sections.translation: nor will I allow it .

sections.label: Section 2.3.405b
sections.translation: But why ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.405c
sections.translation: Because , with those good looks , it would be scandalous if she were to be following a matron when she ’ s walking through the streets ; all people would be staring , gazing , nodding , winking , hissing , twitching , crying out , be annoying , and singing serenades at our door ; my door , perhaps , would be filled with the charcoal marks [*] of her praises ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.410
sections.original: atque , ut nunc sunt maledicentes homines , uxori meae
sections.translation: and , according as persons are scandalizing at the present day , they might throw it in the teeth of my wife and myself , that we are carrying on the business of a Procurer . Now what occasion is there for this ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.412
sections.original: Hercle qui tu recte dicis , et tibí adsentior .
sections.translation: Why , faith , you say what ’ s just , and I agree with you . But what shall be done with her now ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.413b
sections.translation: Exactly ; I ’ ll buy for your mother some stout wench of a female slave , not a bad servant , but of ungainly figure ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.415
sections.original: ut matrem addecet familias , aut Syram aut Aegyptiam :
sections.translation: as befits the mistress of a family — either a Syrian or an Egyptian woman : she shall do the grinding , spin out the yarn , and stand a lashing ; and on her account no disgrace at all will be befalling our doors .

sections.label: Section 2.3.418
sections.original: Quid si igitur reddatur illi unde empta est ?
sections.translation: What then if she is restored to the person of whom she was purchased ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.418b
sections.translation: By no means in the world .

sections.label: Section 2.3.419
sections.original: Dixit se redhibere , si non placeat .
sections.translation: He said that he would take her back , if she didn ’ t suit .

sections.label: Section 2.3.419b
sections.translation: There ’ s no need of that ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.420
sections.original: litigari nolo ego usquam , tuam autem accusari fidem ;
sections.translation: I don ’ t want you to get into litigation , nor yet your honor to be called in question . I ’ troth , I would much rather , if any must be endured , put up with the loss myself , than that disgrace or scandal on account of a woman should be brought upon my house . I think that I am able to sell for you at a good profit .

sections.label: Section 2.3.424_425
sections.original: Dum quidem hercle ne minoris vendas quám ego emi , pater .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , so long , indeed , as you don ’ t be selling her at a less price than I bought her at , father .

sections.label: Section 2.3.426
sections.original: Tace modo : senex est quidam , qui illam mandavit mihi
sections.translation: Do you only hold your tongue ; there is a certain old gentleman who commissioned me to buy one for him of just that same appearance .

sections.label: Section 2.3.427b
sections.translation: But , father , a certain young man commissioned me to buy one for him of just that same appearance that she is of .

sections.label: Section 2.3.429
sections.original: Viginti minis opinor posse me illam vendere .
sections.translation: I think that I am able to dispose of her for twenty minae .

sections.label: Section 2.3.430
sections.original: At ego si velim , iam dantur septem et viginti minae .
sections.translation: But , if I had chosen , there have been already seven-and-twenty minae offered .

sections.label: Section 2.3.431
sections.translation: But I —

sections.label: Section 2.3.431b
sections.translation: Nay , but I , I say —

sections.label: Section 2.3.431c
sections.original: Ah , nescis quid dicturus sum , tace .
sections.translation: But you don ’ t know what I was going to say ; do hold your tongue . I can add three minae even to that , so that there will be thirty .

sections.label: Section 2.3.433
sections.translation: What are you turning yourself towards ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.433b
sections.translation: Towards him who ’ s making the purchase .

sections.label: Section 2.3.433c
sections.translation: Why , where in the world is this person ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.434_435
sections.original: Eccillum video . iubet quínque me addere etiam nunc minas .
sections.translation: Look there , I see him [*] , yonder ( pointing ) ; he ’ s bidding me even still to add five minae .

sections.label: Section 2.3.436
sections.original: Hercle illunc divi infelicent , quisquis est .
sections.translation: By my troth , may the Gods send a curse upon him , whoever he is !

sections.label: Section 2.3.436b
sections.translation: There he is again , making a sign to me , even still , for me to add six minae .

sections.label: Section 2.3.437b
sections.translation: My man is bidding seven minae ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.438
sections.translation: I ’ faith , he shall never this day outdo me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.438b
sections.translation: for her , full weight , father .

sections.label: Section 2.3.439
sections.translation: He ’ s bidding in vain ; I will have her !

sections.label: Section 2.3.439b
sections.translation: But the other one made the first offer .

sections.label: Section 2.3.440
sections.translation: I care nothing for that .

sections.label: Section 2.3.440b
sections.translation: He bids fifty .

sections.label: Section 2.3.440c
sections.translation: No , a hundred ’ s the offer . Can ’ t you desist from bidding against the determination of my mind . I ’ troth , you ’ ll be having an immense profit , in such a way is this old gentleman for whom she ’ s being purchased . He ’ s not in his senses by reason of his love ; whatever you ask , you ’ ll get .

sections.label: Section 2.3.444
sections.original: Certe edepol adulescens ille , cui ego emo , efflictim perit
sections.translation: I ’ faith , that young man , for whom I ’ m purchasing , is assuredly dying with distraction for love of her .

sections.label: Section 2.3.445b
sections.original: Multo hercle ille magis senex , si tu scias .
sections.translation: Troth , very much more so is that old gentleman , if you did but know it .

sections.label: Section 2.3.446
sections.original: Numquam edepol fuit neque fiet ille senex insanior
sections.translation: I ’ faith , that old man never was nor ever will be more distracted with love than that young man , father , to whom I ’ m lending this assistance .

sections.label: Section 2.3.448
sections.original: Quiesce , inquam . istanc rém ego recte videro .
sections.translation: Do be quiet , I tell you ; I ’ ll see to that matter , that it ’ s all right .

sections.label: Section 2.3.448b
sections.translation: How say you —?
